book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
Extracts from the Acts. Session VI.
title: Extracts from the Acts. Session VI.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: Leo the most renowned secretary said: The holy and blessed Synod know how at the last session we examined divers sayings of the God-forsaken heretics, who had brought charges against the holy and spotless Church of the Christians for the setting up of the holy images.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: But to-day we have in our hands the written blasphemy of those calumniators of the Christians, that is to say, the absurd, and easily answered, and self-convicting definition (ὅρον) of the pseudosyllogus, in all respects agreeing with the impious opinion of the God-hated heretics.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: But not only have we this, but also the artful and most drastic refutation thereof, which the Holy Spirit had supervised.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: For it was right that this definition should be made a triumph by wise contradictions, and should be torn to pieces with strong refutations.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: This also we submit so as to know your pleasure with regard to it.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: The holy Synod said: Let it be read.

verses.book: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
verses.text: John, the deacon and chancellor [of the most holy great Church of Constantinople, in Lat. only] read.
